CanonicalRules.Text: The game contains exactly five canonical vocations: Knight, Assassin, Archer, Mage, Cleric.
The game does not use traditional RPG attributes. Progression is skill-based and item-driven only.
All playable vocations use Mana as their sole combat resource.
Combat roles are emergent and derived from equipment and skill configuration, not predefined class roles.
Combat role output must be displayed as numeric performance vectors instead of categorical labels.
Each vocation has strict allowed off-hand equipment defining build identity.
Classes define only base survivability and mana parameters; no scaling attributes exist.
Equipment may modify extended combat parameters including offensive, defensive, elemental, and utility attributes, independently from class base stats.
Cooldown Reduction is an ultra-rare utility modifier restricted mainly to Tier 4 and Tier 5 items, with a global maximum cap of 15%.
Base HP and Base Mana represent class starting values and scale upward with level progression using vocation-specific scaling curves.
Final HP and Mana are calculated from base class values, level scaling, and equipment bonuses.
Character level progression is infinite but follows soft-cap scaling to prevent uncontrolled power creep.
Elemental affinity progression has a hard maximum level of 100.
Character power is structured through four layers: Level (baseline growth), Skill (mastery growth), Gear (main power source), and Elemental specialization (capped at level 100).

Classes.Description: Stoic frontline warriors built for survivability, heavy defense, and high health pools.
Vanish in shadows to strike vitals with high-speed burst physical and critical damage.
Precise marksmanship experts specializing in ranged efficiency and strategic mobility.
Commanders of raw elementals, trading physical resilience for catastrophic magical damage.
Devout support channels specializing in powerful restorative magic, divine protection, and high mana sustain.
